title: 〜かわりに
description: 〜かわりに (代（か）わりに) means "instead of ~" or "in exchange for ~" — it swaps one thing for another, or balances one thing against a trade-off.

content.text: With a noun, use 名詞（めいし）＋の＋かわりに to mean "instead of / in place of ~." With a verb, attach かわりに to the plain form. It marks a substitute: you do B rather than the expected A.

content.japanese: 名詞（めいし）＋の／動詞（どうし）（普通形（ふつうけい））＋かわりに
日光（にっこう）の代（か）わりに、金沢（かなざわ）に行（い）きました。
content.english: Instead of Nikko, I went to Kanazawa.

content.japanese: 肉（にく）のかわりに豆腐（とうふ）を使（つか）います。
content.english: I use tofu instead of meat.

content.japanese: 兄（あに）のかわりに私（わたし）が会議（かいぎ）に出（で）ます。
content.english: I'll attend the meeting instead of my older brother.

content.text: かわりに also expresses a trade-off: you get one thing but give up (or pay with) another. It often balances a good point against a bad one, similar to "but on the other hand."

content.japanese: 手伝（てつだ）ってくれたかわりに、晩（ばん）ご飯（はん）をおごります。
content.english: In exchange for your helping me, I'll treat you to dinner.

content.japanese: 給料（きゅうりょう）は高（たか）いかわりに、忙（いそが）しいです。
content.english: The pay is high, but in exchange it's busy.

content.text: かわりに on Its Own
かわりに can also start a clause by itself as an adverb meaning "instead," referring back to something just mentioned.

content.japanese: コーヒーがなかったので、かわりにお茶（ちゃ）を飲（の）みました。
content.english: There was no coffee, so I drank tea instead.

examples.japanese: 名詞（めいし）＋の／動詞（どうし）（普通形（ふつうけい））＋かわりに
日光（にっこう）の代（か）わりに、金沢（かなざわ）に行（い）きました。
examples.english: Instead of Nikko, I went to Kanazawa.

examples.english: I'll attend the meeting instead of my older brother.

examples.english: In exchange for your helping me, I'll treat you to dinner.

examples.english: The pay is high, but in exchange it's busy.

examples.english: There was no coffee, so I drank tea instead.

headings.text: かわりに on Its Own
